name: USDA Community Facilities Direct Loan & Grant Program
eligibility: Public bodies, nonprofits, and federally recognized tribes in rural areas (populations under 20,000). Funds essential community facilities including food banks, health clinics, and community centers.

name: HUD Community Development Block Grant (CDBG)
funder: U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development
eligibility: Activities must principally benefit low- and moderate-income persons, prevent or eliminate slums or blight, or address urgent community development needs. Administered through state and local governments.

name: AmeriCorps Volunteer Generation Fund
eligibility: Organizations that recruit, manage, and support volunteers. Must demonstrate capacity to expand volunteer engagement. Priority to organizations serving under-resourced communities.

name: Robert Wood Johnson Foundation Health Equity Grants
eligibility: Nonprofits working to advance health equity, address social determinants of health, and build healthier communities. Particular interest in organizations serving communities of color and low-income populations.

name: W.K. Kellogg Foundation Community and Civic Engagement
eligibility: Nonprofits focused on thriving children, working families, and equitable communities. Priority to Michigan, Mississippi, New Mexico, and New Orleans. Work must be community-led and advance racial equity.

name: EPA Environmental Justice Collaborative Problem-Solving Cooperative Agreement
eligibility: Nonprofit organizations in communities facing disproportionate environmental and public health burdens. Must demonstrate collaborative partnerships with government and industry stakeholders.

name: SAMHSA Community Mental Health Services Block Grant
funder: Substance Abuse and Mental Health Services Administration
eligibility: Community mental health centers and nonprofits providing mental health services. Distributed through state mental health authorities. Serves adults with serious mental illness and children with serious emotional disturbances.
deadline: Contact your state mental health authority

name: Walmart Foundation Community Grant Program
eligibility: 501(c)(3) nonprofits in communities where Walmart or Sam's Club operates. Focus areas: hunger relief, sustainability, workforce development, and veterans/military families. Applied through local store managers.

name: Annie E. Casey Foundation Child & Family Grants
eligibility: Primarily organizations in Casey's focus jurisdictions. Funds work to improve outcomes for vulnerable children, youth, and families — especially those involved in child welfare or juvenile justice systems.
deadline: Invitation-only for most programs; check website for open competitions

name: HRSA Rural Health Care Services Outreach Grant
eligibility: Nonprofits providing or coordinating health care services in rural areas. Funds partnerships to expand access to care for underserved rural populations. Organization must be located in a rural area.

name: Kresge Foundation Human Services Program
eligibility: Nonprofits working on economic security, workforce development, and basic needs for low-income Americans. Focus on systems change and policy advocacy alongside direct services. Strong preference for Detroit-area organizations.

name: DOL Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA) Grants
eligibility: Nonprofits and educational institutions providing workforce development services. Must serve adults, dislocated workers, or youth. Administered through state and local workforce development boards.
deadline: Contact your local workforce development board

name: Blue Shield of California Foundation Grants
eligibility: California nonprofits working on domestic violence prevention and response, or expanding access to health care for underserved Californians. Preference for community-based organizations led by people with lived experience.

name: Surdna Foundation Thriving Cultures Grant
eligibility: Nonprofits supporting arts and culture as tools for community development and social change. Must demonstrate genuine community rootedness. Preference for artists and cultural workers from communities most marginalized.

name: FEMA Hazard Mitigation Grant Program (HMGP)
eligibility: State, local, tribal, and territorial governments, and certain nonprofits. Funds long-term hazard mitigation planning and projects following a federal disaster declaration. Nonprofits typically sub-grant through state emergency management agencies.

name: JPMorgan Chase Foundation Advancing Cities
eligibility: Nonprofits in cities where JPMorgan Chase has significant operations. Focus on workforce development, small business growth, financial health for low-income individuals, and neighborhood revitalization.
deadline: Rolling — invitation preferred, unsolicited reviewed quarterly

name: USDA Farmers Market and Local Food Promotion Program
eligibility: Nonprofits, local governments, agricultural cooperatives, and Tribal governments. Funds development and promotion of local and regional food systems, farmers markets, food hubs, and community-supported agriculture.
